highlights.summary: Handle NSPersistentHistoryTokenExpiredError during Core Data history fetch by recovering the stored token and retrying. On token-expired error the code now logs the condition, calls recoverFromExpiredHistoryToken() which clears lastToken and retries fetchPersistentHistoryTransactionsAndChanges(); if retry fails it sets lastToken to the store's current persistent history token and calls viewContext.refreshAllObjects() (commit:3c99ae4cfdd1).
highlights.userFacingImpact: This attempts to restore the persistent-history-based change-merge pipeline when the persistent history token has expired by resetting the stored token and replaying available history; if replay fails it jumps to the current token and refreshes in-memory objects, which should prevent the app from silently stopping history-based updates in that error case. The patch notes that this error can happen after long inactivity or DB updates and that it was reproduced on a device, but does not quantify frequency or guarantee all scenarios are covered, so a human review of runtime behavior is recommended if needed. (pr:nightscout/Trio#1247) (commit:3c99ae4cfdd1) (pr:gordolio/Trio#1247)

items.summary: Serializes BaseAPSManager bolus progress reporter mutations onto processQueue to remove a mutable-state race. The branch originally experimented with a private actor BolusProgressState (commit:aa39571ab9e3) but the final merged changes restore bolusReporter as a processQueue-guarded variable, move cancelBolus to run on processQueue, and ensure create/clear reporter and doseProgressReporterDidUpdate interactions occur on processQueue (commit:3b064ecf6916, commit:5ee0f432d5a1).
items.userFacingImpact: Intended to remove a race condition in APSManager's bolus progress reporting and make reporter lifecycle mutations serialized. The change is internal to APSManager's concurrency handling; no API surface additions are shown in the supplied sources. Because the implementation approach changed during the PR (actor introduced then removed in favour of dispatching onto processQueue), a human reviewer is recommended to validate the final concurrency model and runtime behaviour in the target environment (see pr:gordolio/Trio#1227, commit:aa39571ab9e3, commit:3b064ecf6916).

The patch notes that this error can happen after long inactivity or DB updates and that it was reproduced on a device, but does not quantify frequency or guarantee all scenarios are covered, so a human review of runtime behavior is recommended if needed. items.summary: Reorganize Xcode project entries in Trio.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj. The PBXBuildFile and PBXFileReference sections were updated with many added and reordered Swift source file references (commit:90dd1b0e509c).
items.userFacingImpact: No direct user-visible behavior changes are shown in the project diff; the changes are to the Xcode project file and file references and therefore primarily affect project organization and build configuration (diff:8e10b4701fdc, file:82ae38a95a23). Human review is recommended to confirm build/CI integration and to ensure no files were unintentionally removed or mis-referenced (diff:8e10b4701fdc).

items.summary: Bump APP_DEV_VERSION in Config.xcconfig in two successive commits: 0.8.3.7 → 0.8.3.8 (commit:4c54147dba78) and 0.8.3.8 → 0.8.3.9 (commit:b66cb43c18fc).
items.userFacingImpact: These changes update development-version metadata (APP_DEV_VERSION) in Config.xcconfig and do not modify application logic or runtime behavior based on the provided diffs. If CI or release tooling relies on APP_DEV_VERSION, verify those systems as needed.

includedPullRequests.title: APSManager thread safety fixes (Part 3)
includedPullRequests.body:
Closes the remaining mutable-state race in APSManager around bolus
progress reporting. Final piece split out of the original https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1145 to have its own architectural discussion.

**BolusProgressState**

  The old `bolusReporter` property was a plain mutable `DoseProgressReporter?`
  mutated from a Combine sink on processQueue (bolusTrigger transitions)
  and concurrently from `cancelBolus` (async context). No isolation.

  Replaced with a private `BolusProgressState` actor that owns the
  reporter and serializes add/remove-observer calls.

**Reorder race in the bolusTrigger sink**

  Pre-change, the sink called `createBolusReporter()` or `clearBolusReporter()`
  directly, both of which were sync. Making them async opens a window
  where two unstructured Tasks (one per emission) could reorder against
  each other at the actor — unstructured Tasks enter an actor in
  scheduler order, not the order they were spawned. A fast bolus
  end-then-start transition (real in practice) could land setReporter
  before its matching clear and wipe the fresh reporter.

  Both transitions are now funneled through a single Task per sink
  emission, awaiting in-order at the actor.

**doseProgressReporterDidUpdate**

  The `DoseProgressObserver` protocol method is sync, so the call to
  `clearBolusReporter()` (now async) is wrapped in a Task. The race that
  would normally make this dangerous is exactly what the generation
  token in BolusProgressState protects against.

includedPullRequests.body:
Closes the remaining mutable-state race in APSManager around bolus
progress reporting. Final piece split out of the original https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1145 to have its own architectural discussion.

**BolusProgressState**

  The old `bolusReporter` property was a plain mutable `DoseProgressReporter?`
  mutated from a Combine sink on processQueue (bolusTrigger transitions)
  and concurrently from `cancelBolus` (async context). No isolation.

  Replaced with a private `BolusProgressState` actor that owns the
  reporter and serializes add/remove-observer calls.

**Reorder race in the bolusTrigger sink**

  Pre-change, the sink called `createBolusReporter()` or `clearBolusReporter()`
  directly, both of which were sync. Making them async opens a window
  where two unstructured Tasks (one per emission) could reorder against
  each other at the actor — unstructured Tasks enter an actor in
  scheduler order, not the order they were spawned. A fast bolus
  end-then-start transition (real in practice) could land setReporter
  before its matching clear and wipe the fresh reporter.

  Both transitions are now funneled through a single Task per sink
  emission, awaiting in-order at the actor.

  The `DoseProgressObserver` protocol method is sync, so the call to
  `clearBolusReporter()` (now async) is wrapped in a Task. The race that
  would normally make this dangerous is exactly what the generation
  token in BolusProgressState protects against.

includedPullRequests.title: Refresh stale persistent history token for CoreData
includedPullRequests.body:
When Trio calls `fetchPersistentHistory` it logs errors. One type of
error, the `NSPersistentHistoryTokenExpiredError` error will cause
Trio to stop refreshing data. This error can happen for a few reasons,
like not using Trio of a while or updating the database.

The fix is to refresh all objects manually and reset the persistent
history token, i.e., `lastHistoryToken` stored in UserDefaults.

This error is extremely hard to test, but I had a device stuck in this
state and confirmed the fix.

includedPullRequests.title: Refresh stale persistent history token for CoreData
includedPullRequests.body:
When Trio calls `fetchPersistentHistory` it logs errors. One type of
error, the `NSPersistentHistoryTokenExpiredError` error will cause
Trio to stop refreshing data. This error can happen for a few reasons,
like not using Trio of a while or updating the database.

The fix is to refresh all objects manually and reset the persistent
history token, i.e., `lastHistoryToken` stored in UserDefaults.

This error is extremely hard to test, but I had a device stuck in this
state and confirmed the fix.
